behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE apply: this behavior depends on real filesystem watch semantics for nested paths. A fake event emitter can miss failures where the OS watcher is not actually subscribed to subdirectories. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but the text "placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root" requires validating real watch coverage at the OS boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp input/output directory tree. Create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, write a file into it, and assert exactly one transformed output appears. This requires a real watcher and real filesystem events.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS indicates this is primarily local logic: path+time-window dedup can fail inside one component without real I/O. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP do not apply. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but behavior text explicitly says "deduplication logic" and does not require OS/network/subprocess or model output.
behaviors.test_description: Test the dedup component directly with a controllable clock: submit the same path twice within the configured window and assert first is accepted, second is rejected; then move time past the window and assert acceptance again.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly references OS event behavior, so REAL-INFRA requires System. MOCK-HIDE also applies: synthetic single-event tests can pass while real platforms emit create/change/rename bursts and trigger duplicates. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but "operating system emits multiple filesystem events" disqualifies it.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with a real watched directory and perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., temp file + rename or repeated flushes). Assert the pipeline runs once and only one output artifact is produced for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is an OS-level lifecycle condition (delete/recreate directory while watcher is active). In-process fakes often miss watcher invalidation/rebind issues, so REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE require System. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but "directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running" requires real filesystem/watcher behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real directory, delete that directory, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file. Assert the daemon processes the new file without restarting the process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on interaction between filesystem timestamps, event timing, and queueing across modules. REAL-INFRA is needed to catch failures from timestamp granularity and OS delivery order that fakes can hide. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but "arrive at the same time" and ordering by "creation timestamps" requires real filesystem metadata/timing behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple files in rapid succession in a real watched directory (including near-simultaneous writes), record expected order by filesystem creation timestamp as used by the daemon, and assert observed processing order matches.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This failure boundary is OS/file-write state and watcher timing. MOCK-HIDE applies because mocks usually do not reproduce partial-write timing, file locks, or size-stabilization races. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered, but behavior text "still being written to when the arrival event fires" explicitly requires real filesystem timing.
behaviors.test_description: Open a file in the watched directory and write it in chunks with delays while keeping the write stream open. Assert daemon does not process during partial writes and processes once after write completion/close.
